channel.title: Littoralicious
channel.description: Modern science and regional recipes from the sea. For yacht chefs and serious culinary practitioners.

item.title: The Truth About Paying Tax as Yacht Crew
item.description: What Europeans, Americans, South Africans, and Australians actually owe in tax. The myth of tax-free yachting, CRS reporting, and practical compliance steps.

item.title: How to Become a Yacht Chef: The Real Path Nobody Advertises
item.description: Certifications, day work, crew agencies, what to cook at a trial, salary by vessel size, and what nobody tells you before your first season.

item.title: The Fat Index: Every Cooking Fat Ranked by Science
item.description: Smoke points, omega ratios, oxidative stability — every cooking fat ranked by what the research actually says. A galley-first reference.

item.title: The Salt Index: Every Culinary Salt Ranked by Science
item.description: Mineral content, grain structure, dissolution rate — every culinary salt ranked by what matters in the galley. No marketing, just data.

item.title: The Acid Index: Every Culinary Acid Ranked by Science
item.description: pH, flavour profile, heat stability — every culinary acid ranked for the working galley. Vinegars, citrus, fermented acids, and more.

item.title: Cooked: How Fire Made Us Human and Why the Person Holding the Pan Still Matters
item.description: Cooking drove brain evolution, built social bonds, and shaped every culture on Earth. The anthropological evidence for why chefs matter.

item.title: Sea Urchin: Seasonal Guide, Compound Profile, and Sourcing
item.description: When to buy, where to source, and what the compound profile looks like at peak season. A working reference for sea urchin.

item.title: Sea Urchin Beurre Blanc: Roe as Natural Emulsifier
item.description: Sea urchin roe contains natural phospholipids that stabilise emulsions. How to use it as a beurre blanc base.

item.title: Umami in Western Cuisine: Fermentation as the Universal Glutamate Engine
item.description: Parmesan, soy sauce, miso, fish sauce — fermentation is the universal path to glutamate. The science of umami across culinary traditions.

item.title: Spaghetti ai Ricci di Mare: Mondello, Sicily
item.description: The classic Sicilian sea urchin pasta from Mondello. History, technique, and the coastal tradition behind it.

item.title: Myojaku Earns Three Stars and STCW Anti-Harassment Training Goes Mandatory
item.description: Industry news: Myojaku receives three Michelin stars, and new STCW requirements make anti-harassment training mandatory for all crew.

item.title: The Silver Lining Protocol: A Science-Backed Survival Guide for Galley Sanity
item.description: Mental health, resilience, and crew welfare. Psychology research applied to life at sea.

item.title: The Pharmacy in Your Spice Rack: What Science Actually Knows About Medicinal Plants in Food
item.description: Phytochemistry, bioavailability, and what peer-reviewed research actually says about cooking with medicinal plants.

item.title: Technology in the Galley: Claude Code Is Your Operating System
item.description: How AI tools like Claude Code can streamline galley operations, menu planning, and provisioning workflows.
